title: OpenAI describes GPT-Red, an automated red-team model
friendlyTitle: OpenAI uses one AI to test another AI for safety problems
summary: OpenAI says it trained GPT-Red to find vulnerabilities, especially prompt-injection failures, and then used the resulting attacks to train GPT-5.6. The company reports six times fewer failures on its hardest direct prompt-injection benchmark than its best production model four months earlier.
friendlySummary: OpenAI built GPT-Red to look for ways AI systems can be tricked, then used those tests to train a stronger model to resist them.
whyItMatters: As agents browse, read files and call tools, prompt injection becomes a practical reliability and security problem. Automated adversarial testing could help safety work scale, though OpenAI’s reported results remain vendor claims that need continued independent scrutiny.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could make AI agents safer when they read web pages, emails and files that may contain harmful instructions.
